channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: NEW Claude Design Update Changes EVERYTHING!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses a design update to Claude, claiming it changes everything. The video promotes AI tools and courses for SEO and business automation.

item.title: This NEW Hermes Update Builds Agents From Scratch
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes introduces a Blank Slate mode that lets users build AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only necessary tools. This approach reduces bloat, prevents unwanted feature drift from updates, and simplifies agent configuration for lean use cases.

item.title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Ryo Lu discusses how AI is changing the software building process and the risks of black-box abstraction. The talk emphasizes staying close to material implementation details and preserving craft rather than losing understanding through opaque AI layers. Key concern is whether AI tooling maintains human connection to the underlying system.

item.title: This NEW NotebookLM Update Changes Everything
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Google's NotebookLM has been upgraded to function as a full AI agent capable of writing code, building sources, and delivering finished outputs autonomously. New features include a cloud computer for handling code and documents, auto-source building from initial ideas, and a Studio for generating podcasts and reports with cited answers.

item.title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
item.description: Google for Developers: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities by running 10 independent subagents on a single local machine, with an orchestrator delegating tasks to subagents that autonomously complete work like SVG art generation. The demonstration achieves inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and showcases batch processing with orchestrator-and-subagent task delegation for enterprise and on-prem deployments.

item.title: Claude Code AI SEO: How to Rank #1 with Claude Code
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie presents an agentic SEO system that integrates Claude Code with a user's own Google Search Console data to identify rankable keywords and automatically generate and deploy SEO content across multiple sites. The approach bypasses common keyword tools to find less competitive opportunities, with a demonstrated case of increasing site traffic from 2 to 200+ daily clicks.

item.title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: At Cursor's inaugural Compile conference, CEO Michael Truell announced Cursor Mobile, Origin Git platform, cloud agent updates, and a new model trained from scratch. The platform reports 95% usage is now agents, and Cursor is positioning itself as an agent-native development environment by giving agents their own compute resources.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent Updates Change Everything!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes 0.17 introduces a framework for building teams of autonomous AI agents with async sub-agents, loop engineering for quality control, and an Agent Operating System. The update enables no-code automation and business content automation at scale.

item.title: NEW AI SEO Agent is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie promotes a new AI SEO agent designed for automating search engine optimization tasks. The agent is positioned as a powerful tool integrated with Agent OS and promoted through his AI Profit Lab community.

item.title: Rethinking the ROI of cybersecurity
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology argues that security spending should not impede innovation. The video advocates for strategic risk thinking that balances security protection with business growth and speed to market.

item.title: Are AI Glasses Over? + Big Technology AI Summit Audience Questions
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss whether AR glasses represent a viable AI product category, examining the state of AI devices, form factors, and societal implications. The episode covers Snapchat Specs, the ideal characteristics for AI devices, and geopolitical considerations in AI development.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro LEAKS is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses leaked features of Gemini 3.5 Pro, characterizing them as remarkable. The video promotes his AI-related courses and services including Agent OS and AI SEO strategies.

item.title: Get Fable 5 output on a cheaper model
item.description: Jack Roberts: A leaked Fable 5 system prompt can be injected into Claude Opus 4.8 to achieve near-identical output quality at a fraction of the cost. The approach requires only one line of code and is available on GitHub.

item.title: Sakana Fugu vs Fusion (Beats Fable 5?)
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie compares Sakana's Fugu (Ultra and Mini) with OpenRouter Fusion via side-by-side tests on visual and code generation tasks. Fugu Ultra ranks first for quality, Fusion second, and Fugu Mini third, though Fugu offers cheaper flat pricing with daily caps while Fusion charges per token with easier API access.

item.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
item.description: EO: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone does not create lasting software advantage. Real moats come from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are exaggerated and that the best AI products build deep customer loyalty through raving fan experiences.

item.title: Learn 97% of Claude in Under 12 Minutes
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts walks through seven levels of Claude functionality, from basic chat to autonomous agents and code generation. Coverage includes model selection between Opus and Sonnet, prompt engineering, integrations with tools like Firecrawl and Gmail, sub-agents for parallel task execution, codebase analysis with Graphify, and the Claude Code operating system for spend tracking.

item.title: What caused this React app to crash?
item.description: Google for Developers: Google for Developers presents a CI pipeline puzzle where a React app crashes in production after upgrading from React 18 to React 19, with the root cause hidden in the cache setup. The video invites viewers to diagnose the issue based on build patterns and caching behavior.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent v0.17.0 is INSANE
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes Agent v0.17.0, called "The Reach Release," is an open-source agent framework that integrates across applications with a new Blank Slate setup mode that lets users start with minimal configuration and add only needed features. The release includes iMessage integration, background sub-agents, persistent memory, and desktop upgrades.

item.title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
item.description: Julia Turc: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.

item.title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: Cedric Clyburn argues that while AI promises speed in software development, productivity gains are limited despite faster coding. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows with AI agents to improve testing, outcomes, and delivery.

item.title: Stop Prompting. Start Looping (The New AI Way)
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Loop engineering replaces manual prompt iteration by defining success criteria, letting an agent act, and using a judge model to verify results and repeat until the goal is met. The approach includes several implementations: Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards with planner/builder/reviewer roles, Fusion boardroom combining multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches.

item.title: GLM-5.2 + OpenDesign: SOTA CHEAP DESIGN SYSTEM! This is AWESOME!
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing demonstrates GLM-5.2 combined with OpenDesign to generate high-quality UI prototypes including landing pages, dashboards, and mobile app interfaces. The workflow produces polished HTML and CSS artifacts with consistent design systems through integration via OpenCode and Z.AI's API setup, with OpenDesign installable via desktop app, source, or Docker.

item.title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
item.description: Better Stack: Apple Container Machines is a new feature providing lightweight, persistent Linux environments on Mac, built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It functions similarly to WSL for Windows, enabling developers to test on Linux while developing on macOS with shared home directory access.

item.title: New Agent OS is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Agent OS is an operating system for coordinating multiple AI agents like Hermes and Jarvis in unified workflows. It uses Kanban boards for task triage, supports agent orchestration through group chat and pipelines, and integrates tools like Obsidian and NotebookLM for memory and content generation, with deployment options for SEO automation and other tasks.

item.title: LIVE: Sakana VS Claude Fable 5 VS Fusion!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie tests and compares the Sakana model against Claude Fable 5 and Fusion in a live stream. The session focuses on practical AI applications built with an Agent OS framework for SEO and business automation.

item.title: Sakana: Fable 5 Intelligence WITHOUT Fable 5!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Sakana released Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system with a standard and higher-end "Fugu Ultra" variant that uses panel-style synthesis of multiple model responses. The system is accessible via a single model API and reportedly matches frontier models like Claude and others on benchmarks.

item.title: LIVE: NEW Mythos 5.1?
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO promotes an Agent OS product through a live streaming session. The content focuses on building agentic operating systems and monetizing AI tools for time and cost savings.

item.title: NEW Claude Sonnet 5 coming?
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO creates content discussing rumors or speculation about a potential Claude Sonnet 5 release. The video is paired with promotions for his AI business courses and SEO tools.

item.title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video analysis covering China's MOYA robot with lifelike skin and reactions alongside Boston Dynamics Atlas and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot. The creator argues humanoid robots are transitioning from staged demos to practical real-world tasks in factories, hospitals, and warehouses.

item.title: Fable 5 Just Got Banned, Here’s What To Do
item.description: Jack Roberts: A video argues that model bans don't eliminate capability, they redirect it elsewhere. The creator suggests frontier model restrictions are temporary ecosystem friction and that alternatives like Opus 4.8 remain available and competitive.

item.title: Delegate and schedule tasks in Claude Cowork
item.description: Claude: Claude Cowork is a tool that enables users to delegate work and schedule recurring tasks such as meeting prep and hourly content scans. It is presented as part of Claude's product offerings.

item.title: 3‑2‑1 Backup Rule Explained: Protect Your Data from Disaster
item.description: IBM Technology: The 3-2-1 backup rule is a data resiliency strategy that specifies maintaining three copies of data across two different media types with one copy stored offsite. This approach protects against data failures and ransomware attacks through geographic and media diversity.

item.title: Gemma Coder (Fable Distilled): WHAT.. Fable LIKE OUTPUT from a 12B MODEL?
item.description: AICodeKing: Gemma 4 12B Agentic Fable 5 Compose is a fine-tuned local coding model designed for code generation, tool use, terminal workflows, and agentic tasks. It claims approximately 3.5x improvement over base Gemma 4 12B and can be run locally via Ollama, LM Studio, or llama.cpp with Q4 K M quantization recommended.

item.title: TypeScript is not TypeScript anymore...
item.description: Better Stack: TypeScript 7 rewrites the compiler from TypeScript into Go, achieving approximately 10 times faster type checking. The release candidate introduces shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode while maintaining compatibility with existing error outputs and file formats.

item.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
item.description: AI Engineer: The AI Engineer channel announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, expo floor experiences, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and vertical-specific sessions. The event features $40k in sponsor offers and limited early-bird ticket availability.

item.title: This Is The First Real Shape Of AGI: Fusion Agents
item.description: AI Revolution: The video argues that Fusion Agents and Apps in AI represent the first tangible shape of AGI by demonstrating the ability to execute complex workflows across multiple coordinated agents, moving beyond reasoning to actual tool-building and deployment. These systems can create interactive applications, split complex work across multiple AI workers, and deliver usable outputs integrated with real platforms.

item.title: This OpenSource Repo will 10X Your Hermes Agent
item.description: Jack Roberts: Agent Reach is an open source repo that augments Hermes agents with web research capabilities across YouTube, LinkedIn, Reddit, X, and GitHub, plus data enrichment and contact verification via Clay integration. The system uses intelligent routing and multi-source research to reduce token usage while enabling automated lead discovery.

item.title: Replace Canva with this NEW AI Tool
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates using Claude with a specialized prompt to generate Instagram carousel designs in under 10 minutes, claiming output quality matches or exceeds Canva's. The author argues this approach makes dedicated design tools unnecessary for content creation.

item.title: GLM 5.2 is my new favorite model...
item.description: Better Stack: GLM-5.2 is an open-weights AI model licensed under MIT that ranks first on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. It outperforms GPT-4.5 on the GDPval benchmark and achieved top placement on Design Arena's web design leaderboard, becoming the first model to surpass the Claude line including Claude 3.5.

item.title: GLM 5.2 SUPER MODE: Here's HOW TO USE GLM-5.2 to it's FULL POTENTIAL
item.description: AICodeKing: GLM 5.2 is presented as a major upgrade over GLM 5.1 with strong performance on coding tasks. The video demonstrates using GLM 5.2 with Verdent to build a full creator sponsorship dashboard application from a single prompt, including data persistence, responsive UI, and deployment capabilities.

item.title: This Claude Code Plugin Writes 94% Less Code (ponytail)
item.description: Better Stack: Ponytail is a Claude Code plugin that reduces code generation by enforcing the YAGNI principle, directing AI agents to use native platform features and standard libraries before writing custom code. It claims to cut costs up to 77% while reducing code output by 94%.

item.title: Shocking New AI Just Hit 12 Million Tokens With 1000x Less Compute
item.description: AI Revolution: Subquadratic claims its SubQ model achieves 12 million token context windows with 1000x less attention compute than prior approaches using Sparse Attention Architecture (SSA). The model targets enterprise AI by enabling direct reasoning over full documents, codebases, and contracts without chunking or retrieval augmentation.

item.title: Different Profiles, Different Personalities—A Game Changer
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents content about how different profiles and personalities serve as a meaningful innovation. The video is titled "Different Profiles, Different Personalities, A Game Changer" suggesting system personalization or behavioral differentiation is impactful for AI applications.

item.title: Best AI Logo Generator: How to Make Logo With AI (2026 Comparison)
item.description: AI Master: A hands-on comparison of five AI logo generators: Design.com, BrandCrowd, Canva, Wix Logo Maker, and Vistaprint. The video tests each tool on the same brief to evaluate output quality, AI chat editing capabilities, licensing, and brand kit generation in 2026.

item.title: The data black hole at the center of AI
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel argues that massive data volume is the central, often invisible force holding together AI capabilities. He frames data scale as analogous to a gravitational "black hole" at the center of a galaxy of model capabilities, emphasizing the gulf between human and AI sample efficiency during training.

item.title: Save 70% of your AI Tokens
item.description: Jack Roberts: Graphify is an open source tool that maps a codebase into a knowledge graph, allowing AI systems to reference the graph structure instead of re-reading the entire codebase with each prompt. This approach reduces token consumption by up to 70 percent while improving answer accuracy.

item.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Start Loop Engineering.
item.description: Austin Marchese: Loop Engineering (or Agentic Engineering) is a technique where developers build iterative loops with AI agents instead of relying on single-prompt interactions. The approach requires five building blocks: trigger, execution skills, goal and verification, and output and memory, enabling AI systems to autonomously work toward objectives.

item.title: One Shared #Hermes From Any Device (Local UI, VPS Brain)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed architecture where Hermes can be shared across multiple devices using a local UI connected to a remote VPS-hosted inference backend. This enables local interface access while centralizing model compute resources.

item.title: Why Desktop #Hermes Beats #Telegram for Real Work
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI argues that Desktop Hermes outperforms Telegram for productive work. The video covers sessions, profiles, and artifacts as key features distinguishing Hermes.

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI models & bots dominate the internet
item.description: IBM Technology: A Cloudflare study shows agentic AI bots generate 57.4% of web requests globally. The Mixture of Experts podcast panel debates whether this represents a fundamental shift in web interaction or a continuation of existing bot dominance patterns.
